findings.title: Result validator requires a blocked ref on deny but does not bind each mismatch condition to its specific blocked claim ref.
findings.rationale: precheckControlPlaneHermesValidationTenantBoundary pushes the correct ref per condition and is well-tested, and assertControlPlaneHermesValidationTenantBoundaryPrecheckResult correctly forces precheck_passed===false on any actor/resource/matter-missing/matter-mismatch condition and requires blocked_claim_refs to be non-empty on deny. However, the independent result validator (the fail-closed gate) would still accept a hand-constructed deny result whose blocked_claim_refs do not correspond to the actual mismatch (e.g., an actor_tenant mismatch carrying only the resource ref). Given the project's strong defense-in-depth posture of independent validators re-deriving invariants, this is a minor completeness gap. It does not affect S03 runtime correctness: the prechecker is the sole producer, it is deterministic, and its specific-ref behavior is covered by the deny test matrix. Fixed by binding each detected mismatch condition to its specific blocked claim ref in the independent result validator and adding a tampered deny-result test.

parsed_review.summary: S03 Tenant boundary precheck correctly consumes the S02 normalized hermes_validation request, re-derives actor/resource/Matter tenant boundaries against tenant_id from metadata only, emits allow_metadata_only for genuine same-tenant context and deny_blocked_claim_required with the precise blocked claim refs for actor/resource/Matter mismatch or missing Matter tenant when Matter is present. It carries S01/S02 validation contexts, TEN-001..TEN-008 trace refs, acceptance gates, M00 S02/S03 refs, permission_decision_ref, and audit_event_ref; requires the carried allow_metadata_only precheck pass; and fails closed on non-hermes entrypoints, tampered S02/M00 markers, all eleven forbidden boundary claims, and unknown truthy claims (only undefined/null/false are ignored). No cross-tenant allow is reachable: the allow path requires all four mismatch conditions to be false, and the result validator independently forces deny on any mismatch. The function is a pure, deterministic, frozen-output computation with no service execution, no runtime route creation, no tenant lifecycle mutation, no Hermes validation execution, no Hermes evidence creation, and no product-state writes; Matter trace/permission/audit-hint checks are correctly deferred to S04/S05/S06. Contract, policy, prechecker, fixture, tests, and the contract validator are mutually consistent and exhaustive. No P0/P1/P2 issues; one non-blocking P3 defense-in-depth hardening note on the result validator's ref-to-condition binding. The expected rp00:control-plane:validate FAIL is solely due to S03 production_ready evidence not yet being written, consistent with this pre-review state. This single read-only claude-opus-4-8 effort=max review is sufficient for closeout once evidence is recorded.

p3.finding: Result validator requires a blocked ref on deny but does not bind each mismatch condition to its specific blocked claim ref.
p3.rationale: assertControlPlaneHermesValidationTenantBoundaryPrecheckResult now requires the actor/resource/Matter missing/Matter mismatch-specific blocked claim ref whenever that condition is present, and service.test.js asserts tampered deny results fail closed.

raw_claude_cli_result_summary.summary: Claude returned PASS_WITH_FINDINGS, blocks_subphase_closeout false, blocks_goal_closeout false, with one P3 finding and no P0/P1/P2 findings.
